   44% before



Slide 13

   - Where did we land on which brands should we just acquire local
   distributor?
- For Torridien, the NPC is still very bad?

Slide 14

   SKUs?

Slide 15

   - For TH, # of bands still very little?
- Several brands NPC is quite bad?
For eg. Somebyme?
And Dralthea?

- Seems like consistent brands across different markets



Slide 18

   - Why are we accepting poorer results for 11.11 compared to 10.10?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Following up on *Action Item #7 *from the Oct 23 Bi-weekly regarding *KRCB
Paid Ads Take Rate*, I’d like to provide clarification on the
TH performance.

First, I’d like to apologize for the confusion caused by our Oct 23 update
— when we mentioned that TH & TW “met 100% Take Rate RR,” it referred to
the *run rate of Push Program sellers as of Oct 19*, not the final
month-end performance.

In reality, the October Net Paid Ads Take Rate for TH closed below target,
mainly due to temporary budget shifts by non-program sellers.

However, we expect recovery in November with:

   -

   *Equalberry’s cross-market budget expansion** (+56K USD across 6
   markets)*, and
   -

   *Continuation of the ongoing TH/TW Push Programs*, both of which have
   shown consistent spending ramp-up since early November.

With these adjustments, *TH’s Take Rate is projected to realign with the
November target level*.
You may refer to the detailed breakdown and outlook in the table below for
more context.

Best regards,

Ka Young Choi

Shopee | Marketing Solutions Team

Mobile +82 010-9699-3953 / 02-2063-4518 Ext. 3646

Address 16F, Centerfield West Tower, 231 Teheran-ro, Gangnam-gu, Seoul,
Republic of Korea

MS Net Ads Take-Rate (%) – aligned on the TR% definition and reflected
   this to the budget

For Nov, the KRJP PnL budget is USD 2.1M, with the current RR at 65% (EOM
projection around USD 1.4M).

The gap versus target is expected to narrow significantly after 11.11, as
over 50% of the PC2 shortfall is driven by Net Ads Take-Rate (%) gap, for
which:

the current EOM figure does not yet reflect “deferred” paid ads spending
   to11/11 by KR sellers

On top of 1. the TH/TW Paid Ads Program sellers’ Nov planned spending is
   also not yet captured in the current projection

As of 11/9 the PnL RR% is 84%, with missing target by $350k, majority
   coming from KR MP PC2 from net ads TR% gap which will be recovered with MS
   team’s initiative as mentioned above

KRJP will hit Nov Toline targets(ADO RR 101%, ADG RR 102% respectively)
   thanks to good KRJP 11/11 performance(recording >300k order as of 11/11 5pm
   KST) outpacing the PLT RR%

Accuracy improvement made for net ads TR% target setting as well as GTF

Hi Ian,

I’d like to share an update on KRCB’s November Net Ads performance versus
target.

As of the end-November projection, the Net Ads Take Rate is projected to
close at 6.3% with target RR 98% and spending RR 100% if we achieve 100% of
the seller pitch programs’ spending target for Nov. The current figures
reflect data including 11.11 performance.
[image: image.png]

[image: image.png]

And for each metric :

   -

   Nov spending EOM projection includes MS Team’s pitch plans for Target
   Seller Programs in expanded markets SG, VN, PH starting from Nov, in
   addition to TH, TW. For reference, we achieved 80% of spending target for
   TH and 94% of spending target for TW
   -

   Nov spending MTD actual - We will check data including 11.11 actual and
   share tomorrow due to 2 days data delay

2025년 11월 12일 (수) 오후 11:28, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>님이 작성:

content.question:
Thanks



Has all KR/JP sellers transition to using GMV Max already?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Following up on your questions regarding the GMV Max conversion and the
November Paid Ads MTD performance, please find the updates below.


1. GMV Max Conversion — Update Expected by Nov 18

Most KR sellers have already migrated after the manual mode sunset.
To ensure accuracy across both KR and JP markets, we are currently
cross-validating the dataset and will share the consolidated update by
early next week (ETA: Nov 18).

2. Nov. Paid Ads — MTD Performance (Nov 1–13)

Including the 11.11 results, Net Paid Ads Take Rate MTD stands at *5.25%*,
reaching *82% of target*, while spending is tracking at *117% RR*.


[image: image.png]

The current TR RR% is temporarily below target due to:

   -

   *11.11 spike-day distortion* where GMV denominator increased abnormally
   mid-month
   -

   *Other seller push programs (TH, TW, SG, VN, PH)* have not yet been
   reflected in the MTD data

With these factors normalizing in the coming days, we expect TR% to recover
and stay close to the November target toward month-end.


Thank you,

Ka Young Choi

Shopee | Marketing Solutions Team

Mobile +82 010-9699-3953 / 02-2063-4518 Ext. 3646

Address 16F, Centerfield West Tower, 231 Teheran-ro, Gangnam-gu, Seoul,
Republic of Korea

For local OS onboarding, this week's update on the 13 SHP-TTS brands

   - Sellers tagged as Next Step Action group 2, Local Team can leverage
   current local OS or approach the TTS vendor we newly identified (Glampick
   for cosrx)
   - Sellers tagged as Next Step Action group 3, Local Team can approach
   the TTS vendor we newly identified (Silicon2 for VT), or approach directly
   local TTS shop to identify the vendor since HQ won't disclose (dralthea)
   - Sellers tagged as Next Step Action group 1, sellers committed to
   achieve the NPC and assortment gap KPI by Dec, and therefore rejected
   consenting to local OS. But Local Team can approach directly local TTS shop
   (StoreNgo for Torriden, Silicon2 for isntree)

content.answer:
We as a CB team were unable to convince brands in this 1 week because we
were not clear enough in what we can offer for their local OS, when these
brands are clearly against the dual-shop concept and we need to show them
tangible benefits. That's why I think structurally Local Team should be the
main team to onboard local OS as they can better clarify what SHP can offer
for the local shop, OR let our team know local team's offerings and we can
be the comms channel.

It's very regretful how you say this doesn't solve anything. We got seller
commitment on LFF, NPC and SKU gap KPI, and got vendor info & operating
model, and clarified what the next steps can be which will close SHP-TTS
gap. If it's a wholesale model, vendors have good negotiating power when
speaking with the brand. For this 1 week, we talked to the business owner
of the overall market (MY) or at least SHP-MY. And dralthea, we talked to
B2B team who is more keen to increase the local vendors' volume, as opposed
to brand HQ CB shop. The brands we talked to B2C teams include below and we
can follow up to get to the right PIC within their org.

   - Wishtrend : TTS vendor already onboarded to shopee local. We can
   follow up on this shop
   - Grafen : TTS selling only close-to-expiry items with shop ADO of 0-2.
   - Roundlab : But plan to not operate as local vendor model for TTS
   either. All platform shops to run via brand entity
   - Dalba


If our goal is to onboard local OS, and not close the gap with TTS, then
what we did at least provided vendor info to get us started. If this effort
doesn't start solving either local OS open or TTS gap close, then you let
us know what we do.

We as a CB team were unable to convince brands in this 1 week because we
were not clear enough in what we can offer for their local OS, when these
brands are clearly against the dual-shop concept and we need to show them
tangible benefits. That's why I think structurally Local Team should be the
main team to onboard local OS as they can better clarify what SHP can offer
for the local shop, OR let our team know local team's offerings and we can
be the comms channel.

It's very regretful h

were not clear enough in what we can offer for their local OS, when these
brands are clearly against the dual-shop concept and we need to show them
tangible benefits. That's why I think structurally Local Team should be the

for the local shop, OR let our team know local team's offerings and we can

content.question: The local vendors need the brands to agree to onboard?

How will your
suggestion solve this?

How will we solve the issue of brands like Torridien
not having TT assortment on our platform?

You mentioned this.



   - Unlike CN or global brands, KR brands open TTS via local vendor
   because they cannot open shop under Korean entity as per TT policy. But
   brands have concerns about the profitability and price control of TTS run
   by local vendors, so they push back on these vendors running SHP local OS

So shall we change our policy so the brands will allow local vendors to
onboard?

content.answer:
Any official shop needs brands' consent and TTS local vendor needs brands'
consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are difficult to come from
only us, the CB team, even if we do represent and negotiate on behalf of
the platform.

For Torriden, our team successfully negotiated with the brand, with the
"push / pressure" of opening local OS, to match the SKUs by Dec and this is
shared in the table.

We can change our policy so that brands need to open local OS through local
entities, like how TTS does. Then brands would likely leverage their
current vendor network  (like TTS vendor) to open local OS. If our goal is
open local OS, this will solve the issue.

Any official shop needs brands' consent and TTS local vendor needs brands'
consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

We have been talking about closing the gap for months, so for the past 2
months, so the team built dashboards for each of NPC, SKU, campaign
participation, and did manual checks to match shop and sku better, because
we understand how inaccurate price data hinders price match progress and
deteriorates SHP-seller relationship.
The NPC, campaign, SKU, we're monitoring and improving without you having
to ask, and we have been sharing in the biweekly to show how much it's
improving for the past months. I believe and I do see how the team did work
to move towards the target and have shown progress, and we don't deserve to
hear that this is not moving anywhere

We show you the NPC, we show you the campaign participation, and we show
you the SKU assortment but if biweekly meeting of Q&A is not sufficient, I
attached dashboard links below that we're using to monitor so you can see
real time as well

   - Campaign participation Oct to Nov KR 84% --> 87%. JP 59% --> 72% (vs
   CN 75% --> 70%) (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TK2R8R6aHpjs-Ay14hJqLdpZHqliaK-M3QKdAbgEc38/edit?gid=927180518#gid=927180518>on
   RM/team level too)
   - SKU exclusive on TTS 108 --> 100 (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/14oaoZg6NxM1erH6ufwVuyYXuAbFBtmF83scxNeew-ns/edit?gid=717011053#gid=717011053>
   )
   - NPC 36% --> 9% (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1dIpCJsO2Gm_yu0Z_eEPWkjGLcoubeIicQLnJoZfONPc/edit?gid=253123941#gid=253123941>)
   NPC is not improving thus we shared seller nego commitments for EOY
   - Seller mgmt benchmark too, we're making progress with actual
   dashboards and SOPs being launched (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bsglDc5mJax9uC7k66vQ9varTa2taFurak0LuqB6E_A/edit?gid=1286813728#gid=1286813728>
   )

For local OS onboarding, let me breakdown into more granular level and
write down which week/day. I will share this this Thursday.

The newsarticle means TT will allow CB model like ours
